properties.title: South-face beds
properties.detail: SSE-facing bench near 1890 ft, ~22° — thermal sun and a downhill escape; Penn State collar deer bed exactly this.
properties.windNote: Beds hold a NNW wind. Approach from the ridge above. Thermals: at dawn scent drains SSE (downhill); by midday it rises NNW (upslope) — cross-check that against the wind.

properties.title: Saddle crossing
properties.detail: A ridge saddle near 2150 ft — deer cross ridges at their low gaps, funneling travel between bedding areas. Prime stand.
properties.windNote: Sit the downhill side on a crosswind. Thermals: scent sinks downhill at dawn/dusk, rises upslope by midday — let the active thermal, not just the wind, pick your side.

properties.title: Bench trail (1701 ft)
properties.detail: A near-level shelf on a steep hillside — deer sidehill along benches like this at a constant elevation to save energy, traveling the lower slopes parallel to the ridge rather than climbing over the top.
properties.windNote: Deer walk it into the wind; sit the downwind end, above or below the trail. Thermals: scent sinks downhill at dawn/dusk, rises upslope by midday — let the active thermal, not just the wind, pick your side.

properties.title: Draw travel corridor
properties.detail: A drainage carrying bed-to-feed travel and your quietest, lowest-scent way in.
properties.windNote: Thermals: scent sinks downhill at dawn/dusk, rises upslope by midday — let the active thermal, not just the wind, pick your side. Hunt the lower end at dawn, the upper end midday.
